name: Seafood Stuffed Pork Tenderloin with Shrimp Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the pork loin down the center and season with salt, red pepper, and black pepper.
2. Heat 3/4 stick of butter in a pan. Sauté chopped green onions, parsley, garlic, and oregano for five minutes. Add crawfish tails and sauté for another five minutes.
3. Place the pork loin on foil and pour the onion mixture into the center. Tie with string to hold the filling in place and fold up the sides of the foil. Place on top of the grill, cover, and add two handfuls of wet mesquite chips to hot coals.
4. Prepare the basting sauce by heating 1/2 stick butter, honey, Worcestershire sauce, and water together.
5. After the loin begins to brown, baste every 10 minutes with the prepared sauce.
6. Prepare the shrimp sauce by heating 1/2 stick of butter. Sauté chopped onions, green onions, and parsley for five minutes. Add thyme, white pepper, salt, oregano, Tabasco sauce, Worcestershire sauce, shrimp, and crawfish tails. Cook for three more minutes.
7. Remove the loin from the grill and cut into 1-inch slices. Pour a portion of shrimp sauce over each slice and enjoy!
